markdown:
#  View Holds on a Subcontractor Invoice as an Invoice Contact for a Payee 
#####  General Availability in Select Markets (United States)
![Flag Us](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/eb8f21ce49403a47d7bb5a0cedc7e0e9/7c0ec0977e654cc6bc9f3d8efa80fbe3/flag-us.png?fm=webp&h=14) [Procore Pay ](https://www.procore.com/pay) is available in the United States. It is designed for General Contractors and Owner-Builders who act as their own General Contractors on a job. Procore Pay extends the [Invoice Management](https://www.procore.com/invoice-management) functionality in the Procore web application to handle the payment process between general and specialty contractors.
##  Background 
With Procore Pay, a Payor can apply manual holds to Subcontractor Invoice payments for a variety of reasons. These include quality issues, such as when delivered work is subpar or warranty support is uncertain. Holds might also be applied for incomplete work, work not meeting the specified quality level, contract disputes, financial concerns, or compliance issues. Whatever the reason for applying a hold, payors can apply these hold types on subcontractor invoices:
  * **Invoice Holds**. Payors can create an invoice hold to alert their users about a hold on a specific subcontractor invoice, so they don't pay it.
  * **Vendor Holds**. Payors can apply a vendor hold to an invoice to their users to pause payments on all of your company's invoices.


To view a hold as an invoice contact, it must be shared with you by the payor.
##  Things to Consider 
  * [Required User Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)


##  Prerequisites 
  * To view a hold as an invoice contact, the payor must mark the **Make Visible to Subcontractor?** check box on the hold. See [Create and Apply a Manual Hold on an Invoice](https://v2.support.procore.com/process-guides/payor-setup-guide/create--apply-payment-holds).


##  Steps 
  1. Navigate to the project's **Commitments** tool.
  2. In the **Contracts** tab, locate the commitment.
  3. Click the **Number** link to open it.
  4. Click the **Invoices** tab.
  5. Locate the invoice to view in the **Invoices** table.
  6. Click the **Invoice #** link.
  7. Click the **Holds** tab. 
#####  Tip
**Don't see any holds?** The Holds tab only displays when one (1) or more holds are shared by the payor. To view the invoice and shared holds as an invoice contact Procore, the payor must grant you 'Standard' or 'Read Only' permissions on the project's Commitments tool. See [What is an invoice contact?](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-is-an-invoice-contact)
![Commitments Holds Invoice Contact](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/d28ea6aefd70864ddc5270452b9b2eb8/122c73e8278bcd65ce233391327196bb/commitments-holds-invoice-contact.png?fm=webp)
  8. If the hold includes an attachment, click the attachment link to download the file to your computer's download location.


## See Also
  * [View Payment Requirements as the Invoice Contact for a Payee](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/view-payment-requirements-as-the-invoice-contact-for-a-payee)
  * [Create and Apply a Manual Hold on an Invoice](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/tutorials/create-and-apply-manual-hold-on-an-invoice)
  * [Procore Pay - Permissions](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/procore-pay/permissions)


In Procore Pay, a _payor_ is a general contractor who sends invoice payments to specialty contractors.
A Subcontractor Invoice is a request for payment submitted by a business or individual who has entered into a binding agreement with another contracting party. In Procore, a subcontractor invoice is an equivalent term for a payable invoice.
title: View Holds on a Subcontractor Invoice as an Invoice Contact for a Payee
description: How to view holds on a subcontractor invoice as a payee's invoice contact.
